@graph.name: Frost Day Prediction in Vienna
@graph.description: A fully documented, open-science machine learning experiment predicting frost days in Vienna using historical meteorological data from GeoSphere Austria (Messstationen Tagesdaten v2, station Wien-Hohe Warte, 2000–2023). A frost day is defined as any day where the minimum temperature drops below 0 °C. A Random Forest Classifier is trained on daily weather features (mean, minimum, and maximum air temperature; precipitation; sunshine hours; relative humidity; horizontal visibility) using Python 3.11, scikit-learn, pandas, and NumPy, following the CRISP-DM methodology. Input data are openly available under CC0. All outputs – trained model, evaluation figures, and metrics – are publicly archived with persistent identifiers following FAIR principles.

@graph.name: Creative Commons Zero 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0 Public Domain Dedication)
Messstationen Tagesdaten v2 (GeoSphere Austria)
@graph.description: Daily meteorological station data from GeoSphere Austria. Parameters used in this experiment: daily mean air temperature (tl_mittel), maximum air temperature (tlmax), minimum air temperature (tlmin), precipitation sum (rr), sunshine duration (so_h), mean relative humidity (rf_mittel), and mean horizontal visibility (vv_mittel). Station: Wien-Hohe Warte. Period: 2000–2023.

@graph.name: Trained Random Forest Classifier – Frost Day Prediction Vienna v1
@graph.description: Trained Random Forest Classifier (scikit-learn 1.5.2) for frost day prediction in Vienna, serialised with joblib (model_random-forest_v1.joblib). Trained on GeoSphere Austria daily meteorological data (Wien-Hohe Warte, 2000–2023). Features: mean, maximum, and minimum temperature; precipitation; sunshine hours; relative humidity; visibility; month; day-of-year. Deposited in TU Wien Research Data Repository (TUWRD test instance).

@graph.name: Evaluation Outputs – Frost Day Prediction Vienna v1
@graph.description: Evaluation outputs of the Random Forest Classifier for frost day prediction: confusion matrix (fig_confusion-matrix_*.png), ROC-AUC curve (fig_roc-curve_*.png), feature importance chart (fig_feature-importance_*.png), and evaluation metrics (metrics_*.csv, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, hyperparameters, and dataset split sizes). Deposited in TU Wien Research Data Repository (TUWRD test instance).

@graph.name: README
@graph.description: Main documentation file describing the experiment, file structure, reproduction instructions, contributors, and links to all artefacts.

@graph.name: 01 – Create DBRepo Tables
@graph.description: Jupyter notebook that creates the relational database tables station and daily_observation in DBRepo. Implements the 3NF schema defined in docs/schema.sql and loads GeoSphere Austria station data.

@graph.name: 02 – Unit Mapping
@graph.description: Jupyter notebook that maps and verifies measurement units from the GeoSphere Austria source columns (e.g. tl_mittel, tlmax, tlmin, rr, so_h, rf_mittel, vv_mittel) to the schema column names used in DBRepo.

@graph.name: 02 – Load Data from DBRepo
@graph.description: Python script that loads meteorological data from the DBRepo REST API using HTTP Basic Authentication. Handles pagination, error checking, and derives computed columns (obs_month, obs_day_of_year, is_frost_day). Returns feature matrix X and label vector y for model training.

@graph.name: 03 – Create DBRepo Views
@graph.description: Jupyter notebook for creating and verifying analytical SQL views in DBRepo: vw_ml_daily_features, vw_ml_complete_cases, vw_ml_balanced_training_sample, vw_monthly_frost_summary. Documents which views were successfully created and which required workarounds.

@graph.name: 04 – Train Model
@graph.description: Python script implementing the full CRISP-DM training pipeline: loads meteorological data from DBRepo via src/02_load-data-from-dbrepo.py, performs a 70/15/15 stratified train/validation/test split, scales features with StandardScaler, tunes hyperparameters (n_estimators, max_depth) via 5-fold stratified cross-validation, trains the final Random Forest Classifier, evaluates it on the test set (accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC), and saves all outputs to outputs/.

@graph.name: Confusion Matrix (2026-05-30)
@graph.description: Confusion matrix of the Random Forest Classifier evaluated on the held-out test set (15 % of total data). Axes show actual vs. predicted class labels (Frost / No frost).

@graph.name: ROC-AUC Curve (2026-05-30)
@graph.description: ROC curve with AUC score for the Random Forest Classifier on the held-out test set, showing the trade-off between true positive rate and false positive rate across classification thresholds.

@graph.name: Feature Importance Chart (2026-05-30)
@graph.description: Horizontal bar chart of Random Forest feature importances (mean decrease in impurity) for all input features: mean, maximum, and minimum temperature; precipitation; sunshine hours; relative humidity; visibility; month; day-of-year.

@graph.name: Evaluation Metrics (2026-05-30)
@graph.description: CSV file containing test-set evaluation metrics for the trained Random Forest Classifier: accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, ROC-AUC, best hyperparameters (n_estimators, max_depth), and dataset split sizes (train_rows, test_rows).

@graph.name: Model Card – Random Forest Classifier for Frost Day Prediction
@graph.description: Model Card for the trained Random Forest Classifier following the Mitchell et al. (2019) format. Covers model description, intended use, out-of-scope uses, training data (with DOI), evaluation results (accuracy 0.971, precision 0.946, recall 0.869, F1 0.906, ROC-AUC 0.995 on held-out test set), limitations, ethical considerations, and licence.

@graph.name: Database Schema (SQL)
@graph.description: Relational schema definition for the Frost Day Prediction in Vienna database in Third Normal Form (3NF). Defines tables station and daily_observation with primary keys, foreign key constraint, and column comments linking each column to the GeoSphere Austria source parameter.

@graph.name: SQL View Definitions
@graph.description: Intended SQL definitions for four analytical views: vw_ml_daily_features (join with derived columns), vw_ml_complete_cases (complete-case filter), vw_ml_balanced_training_sample (class-balanced sample via CTE and ROW_NUMBER), and vw_monthly_frost_summary (monthly aggregates). Authoritative specification; not all views could be created natively in DBRepo.

@graph.name: Entity–Relationship Diagram
@graph.description: Documentation of the entity–relationship diagram for the 3NF database schema, including design decisions for the two-table split, composite primary key, and materialised frost-day view.

@graph.name: Data Management Plan
@graph.description: Data Management Plan (DMP) for the Frost Day Prediction in Vienna experiment, covering data sources, storage, sharing, and long-term preservation.

@graph.name: Measurement Mappings Diagram
@graph.description: Visual diagram showing the mapping between GeoSphere Austria source parameter names and the column names used in the DBRepo schema.

@graph.name: Semantic Mapping (T2.2)
@graph.description: Documentation of the semantic mapping for Work Package 2, Task T2.2, linking source data fields to ontology terms or controlled vocabularies.

@graph.name: DBRepo View Creation Limitations
@graph.description: Documents which SQL features (CTEs, window functions, GROUP BY aggregation, computed columns) were not supported by the DBRepo view builder and describes the workarounds applied in the Python loader.

@graph.name: WP2 Person D Report
@graph.description: Report documenting the Work Package 2 contributions by Person D (Nayma).
